# Loan Tracker

A personal finance tool for tracking multiple loans, visualising repayment schedules, and planning payoff strategies. Built as a standalone HTML file — no server, no build step, no dependencies to install.

![Overview & Loan Cards](data:image/png;base64,)

---

## Features

- **Dynamic loan management** — add or remove loans at any time; each loan can be flexible (proportional budget split) or fixed-payment (minimum guaranteed, surplus redistributed)
- **Two planning modes** — set a fixed monthly payment and see how long it takes, or set a target number of months and get the required monthly amount
- **Monthly check-ins** — check off each month as you pay; remaining balances update in real time across all loans
- **Live charts** — balance-over-time line chart and stacked monthly payment bar chart, both rebuilt on every change
- **Full repayment schedule** — paginated month-by-month table showing per-loan payments and running balances
- **PDF export** — one-click export of the complete report (summary, charts, and schedule) as a formatted PDF
- **Light / dark mode** — toggle in the header; PDF always exports in light mode for clean print output
- **Dynamic start month** — set any month/year as the start of your plan

![Charts & Schedule](data:image/png;base64,)

---

## File structure

```
loan-tracker/
├── index.html          ← markup only — no inline styles or scripts
├── css/
│   ├── base.css        ← CSS variables, reset, animations
│   ├── components.css  ← all UI components (cards, modal, table, charts)
│   └── layout.css      ← page structure and responsive breakpoints
└── js/
    ├── state.js        ← all app state, constants, and shared helpers
    ├── schedule.js     ← payment schedule calculation (pure logic)
    ├── render.js       ← all DOM rendering functions
    ├── controls.js     ← theme, mode, sliders, checkboxes, date picker
    ├── modal.js        ← add / remove loan modal
    ├── export.js       ← PDF generation
    └── app.js          ← entry point, bootstraps the app
```

Also included: `loan-tracker.html` — a fully self-contained single-file version with all CSS and JS inlined, identical in functionality to the project folder.

---

## How to run

### Option A — Single file (simplest)
1. Download `loan-tracker.html`
2. Double-click it — it opens in any modern browser
3. No internet connection required after the first open (fonts and Chart.js are fetched once from CDN, then cached)

### Option B — Project folder
1. Download and unzip `loan-tracker.zip`
2. Open `loan-tracker/index.html` in any modern browser
3. All JS and CSS files are loaded locally — no build step needed

> **Note:** Because the project loads separate JS/CSS files, some browsers block local file:// requests for security. If the page looks unstyled or broken, either use the single-file version (`loan-tracker.html`) or serve the folder with a simple local server:
> ```bash
> # Python 3
> cd loan-tracker
> python3 -m http.server 8080
> # then open http://localhost:8080
> ```

---

## Adding your loans

![Add Loan Modal](data:image/png;base64,)

Click **Add loan** in the header (or the `+` card in the Loans section). Fill in:

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| Loan name | Any label, e.g. "Apartment", "Car", "Personal loan" |
| Remaining balance | The current outstanding amount in EGP |
| Notes / frequency | Optional label shown on the card, e.g. "Monthly · Bank" |
| Fixed monthly payment | Check this if the loan has a contracted minimum payment (e.g. a bank instalment). The fixed amount is always paid first; any surplus budget from your monthly target is then split across all loans. |
| Color | Pick from 8 colors to visually distinguish loans in charts and cards |

To remove a loan, click the **×** button on its card.

---

## Payment modes

### Fixed monthly amount
Set how much you pay each month. The schedule shows how many months until every loan reaches zero. Drag the slider or type directly in the input — the schedule, charts, and month counter all update instantly.

### Target months to finish
Set how many months you want to be debt-free in. The tool calculates the exact monthly payment required and updates the overview.

---

## Marking payments

The **Mark payments made** section shows one checkbox per month. Checking a month:
- Marks it as paid in the schedule table (shown with ✓ and a green tint)
- Deducts that month's payment from the "Paid (checked)" counter
- Updates remaining balances on each loan card
- Does **not** change the payment amounts for future months

Use **Clear all** to reset all checkboxes.

---

## PDF export

Click **Export PDF** in the header. The export includes:
- A summary bar (monthly payment, total remaining, paid so far, months left)
- Loan breakdown table (original, paid, remaining, progress % per loan)
- Both charts as a snapshot image
- The full repayment schedule, paginated across as many pages as needed

The PDF always renders in light mode regardless of your current theme setting.

---

## Pre-loaded loans

The tracker comes pre-loaded with three loans from the original plan:

| Loan | Balance | Type |
|---|---|---|
| Apartment | EGP 2,274,032 | Flexible (proportional) |
| Car Murabaha | EGP 1,183,945 | Fixed at EGP 69,645/mo |
| Personal loan | EGP 21,117 | Fixed at EGP 5,519/mo |

These can be removed and replaced with any loans you like.

---

## Browser support

Works in any modern browser: Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge. No Internet Explorer support.
